mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts signal synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and real estate, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and lifts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Spearman rank correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 40% of rolling 24-month windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index indicates supply-side bottlenecks or capacity utilization declines, which compresses profit margins and forces equity multiples to contract, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in over 60% of observations where INDPRO declines month-over-month, or the signal exhibits no predictive power (IC < 0.02) across any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, it confirms an economic contraction that triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off portfolio rebalancing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy exceeds 55% in the opposite direction, or 60-day forward returns following the trigger are statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained business hiring and economic momentum, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal's 20-day forward return differential between positive and negative payroll growth months is less than 0.5%, or the rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the consumer price index and the federal funds rate indicate active monetary tightening amid persistent inflation, which compresses equity risk premiums and forces multiple contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS increase signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC reduction < 2) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent over three consecutive months signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, which precedes broader economic slowdown and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (IC < 0.01) when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate regression.
